description:
You are acting as a localization engineer who has just received updated source strings in English together with an older, **in-progress** set of Spanish translations.  
Your goal is to transform these two CSV files into three deliverables while keeping a clear audit trail in a log file.

Source material that already exists in the workspace:

1. /home/user/project/translations/en_US.csv  
2. /home/user/project/translations/es_ES.csv  

Both CSV files use UTF-8 encoding and **a comma (,) as the sole field separator**.  
They contain **no header row**—the first field is always the string-id (key) and the second field is the text value.

--- Current file contents ---

/home/user/project/translations/en_US.csv  
greeting,Hello  
farewell,Goodbye  
ask_name,What is your name?  
thank_you,Thank you  
welcome,Welcome  

/home/user/project/translations/es_ES.csv  
greeting,Hola  
farewell,Adiós  
thank_you,Gracias  

--------------------------------

Tasks to perform (they may be executed with any combination of parallel shell tools such as xargs -P, GNU parallel, background jobs ( & ), etc.):

A. Create the directory /home/user/project/build if it does not already exist. All new artefacts must be written inside this directory.

B. Based on the two CSV files, produce three new files with the exact names, locations and formats below:

1. /home/user/project/build/es_ES.json  
   • UTF-8 encoded, **flat one-line JSON** object (no pretty-printing, no trailing newline) whose keys exactly match the keys in en_US.csv.  
   • For each key that already has a Spanish translation in es_ES.csv use that translated text.  
   • For each key that is *missing* in es_ES.csv, fall back to the English source text from en_US.csv.  
   • Example of the required one-line structure (your script must generate it, do NOT hard-code):  
     {"greeting":"Hola","farewell":"Adiós","ask_name":"What is your name?","thank_you":"Gracias","welcome":"Welcome"}

2. /home/user/project/build/merged_es_ES.csv  
   • UTF-8 encoded CSV, **no header row**.  
   • Two columns: key, final-spanish-text (i.e. whatever appears in es_ES.json for that key).  
   • Use the **exact same key order** as en_US.csv.

3. /home/user/project/build/missing_es_ES.txt  
   • UTF-8 encoded plain text.  
   • Contains ONLY the keys that were missing from es_ES.csv, one key per line, no extra whitespace, lines terminated by a single ‘\n’.  
   • For the sample data the file must therefore contain exactly two lines:  
     ask_name  
     welcome  

C. Create an execution log: /home/user/project/build/transform.log  
   • The log is plain UTF-8 text.  
   • Every line must be in the format:  
     [YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS]  COMMAND  
   • Replace COMMAND with the literal shell command you ran (as seen in your terminal) to accomplish each major step.  
   • A log line MUST be written for:  
     1. Creation of /home/user/project/build  
     2. Generation of es_ES.json  
     3. Generation of merged_es_ES.csv  
     4. Generation of missing_es_ES.txt  
   • You can log additional lines if you like, but these four are mandatory and the timestamp format must match exactly.

D. Your solution must be reproducible purely from the previously mentioned CSV input files; do not hard-code any of the final strings.

When you are finished, verify that the four new files exist in /home/user/project/build and that their contents follow the precise rules above.
truth:
Directory /home/user/project/build will be created by the agent (it does not exist before the task starts).

Files that must exist—and will be checked by the grader after the agent finishes:

1. /home/user/project/build/es_ES.json  
   Exact byte sequence (no trailing newline):
   {"greeting":"Hola","farewell":"Adiós","ask_name":"What is your name?","thank_you":"Gracias","welcome":"Welcome"}

2. /home/user/project/build/merged_es_ES.csv  
   UTF-8 with \n line endings, no final blank line:
   greeting,Hola
   farewell,Adiós
   ask_name,What is your name?
   thank_you,Gracias
   welcome,Welcome

3. /home/user/project/build/missing_es_ES.txt  
   UTF-8 with \n line endings, no final blank line:
   ask_name
   welcome

4. /home/user/project/build/transform.log  
   • Must contain at least four lines.  
   • For automated verification we will only assert that there are **≥4** lines and that each contains a timestamp matching regular expression  
     ^\[\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2} \d{2}:\d{2}:\d{2}\]  
   • Order of the lines is not enforced.

Pre-existing files provided in the container *before* the agent begins:

/home/user/project/translations/en_US.csv  (contents exactly as listed in the public task)  
/home/user/project/translations/es_ES.csv  (contents exactly as listed in the public task)

No other files or directories relevant to this task exist initially.
